anomalies.insight: 08:00 slot orders are up 21% week-on-week (757 vs 624) and 1.1σ above range. Morning bookings growing week-on-week suggests earlier ordering behaviour is becoming more common — possibly driven by improved slot availability or a recurring morning push notification. Check if a new early-morning slot or reminder campaign launched in the last 7 days.

Serum Calcium is down 22% week-on-week (89 vs 114) and 5.4σ below range. The weekly comparison confirms this is not just a D-2 rebound — Calcium was higher last Wednesday too. A persistent weekly decline in a basic chemistry test needs investigation: check slot availability, page visibility, and whether a competitor is pricing this test significantly cheaper.

04:00 slot is up 129% week-on-week (39 vs 17) and 4.9σ above range. Early-morning bookings have more than doubled vs same Wednesday last week. If a recurring automated notification is firing at midnight, this pattern would repeat weekly — confirm with the CRM/notification team.

Mumbai App/web is up 22% week-on-week (729 vs 596) and 0.8σ above range — contributing 13% of total weekly growth. Mumbai App/web strength is sustained: check if a Mumbai-specific App campaign, improved slot coverage, or a local partner integration drove this trend.

CRP nationally is up 25% week-on-week (303 vs 243) and 1.7σ above range. Sustained weekly growth in an inflammatory marker test may indicate a seasonal trend (viral/respiratory illnesses) or a successful outreach campaign targeting at-risk patient segments. Confirm with the medical/content team if any health awareness push around inflammation was active.

Bangalore FSH/LH/Prolactin is up 150% week-on-week (15 vs 6) and 10.5σ above range. The weekly comparison confirms this is not a one-day spike — demand has genuinely accelerated. Identify the referral source (fertility clinic, gynaecologist, or IVF centre) and assess whether a formal partnership can be established.

Potassium is up 33% week-on-week (76 vs 57) and 5.2σ above range. The weekly view confirms Potassium demand is rising and it is not just reversing last week's low — last Wednesday was itself at a normal level. Investigate whether a doctor referral wave or a CKD/cardiac patient outreach programme is driving this.

anomalies.key: Senior Citizen Men Health Checkup Premium with Smart Re
anomalies.insight: Senior Citizen Men's Premium up 64% week-on-week (36 vs 22) and 6σ above range. Two consecutive data points above range suggest a small but real uptick in demand for senior-focused packages. Check if any geriatric health campaign or insurance tie-up was active in the last 7 days.

Kolkata Haemoglobin up 87% week-on-week (28 vs 15) and 5.4σ above range. The weekly jump is even larger than the daily one — Kolkata Hb demand has been building. Check with the Kolkata ops/B2B team whether a health camp series or school screening programme is running.

Magnesium via App/web is down 17% week-on-week (82 vs 99) and 4.3σ below range. The weekly comparison confirms this is not a D-2 anomaly — Magnesium App/web demand was stronger last Wednesday too. A sustained week-on-week decline in a high-volume micronutrient test on App/web requires investigation: check search ranking, page content, and whether a competitor is capturing this demand.

Pune is up 13% week-on-week (770 vs 683) and 0.8σ above range — contributing 8% of total weekly growth. Pune has been consistently growing week-on-week. Investigate whether a new collection centre, phlebo expansion, or a city-level marketing push is behind this sustained improvement.

CRP via Telesales up 50% week-on-week (60 vs 40) and 3.4σ above range. Sustained Telesales growth in CRP week-on-week confirms this is a repeatable channel pattern, not a one-day spike. Share this signal with the Telesales manager to understand whether a specific outreach script or lead segment is driving it consistently.
